title: Renewable Energy Policy
description: What are the major policy mechanisms that have successfully increased renewable energy adoption in European countries since 2010?
narrative: The user wants detailed information on government policies—such as feed‑in tariffs, auction schemes, subsidies, tax incentives, and regulatory frameworks—that have demonstrably boosted the share of renewable electricity in Europe after 2010. Relevant documents should describe specific policies, implementation dates, quantitative outcomes, and comparative effectiveness across European nations. Sources that only discuss technology, private‑sector projects, or policies outside Europe are not relevant.

title: Academic Collaboration Tools
description: What features are most important for a digital platform that supports interdisciplinary research collaboration?
narrative: The user seeks scholarly sources—such as journal articles, conference papers, systematic reviews, or white papers—that identify, evaluate, or propose essential features for digital tools used by researchers collaborating across disciplines. Relevant content should discuss functionality like version control, data sharing, real‑time communication, citation management, privacy/security, and integration with research workflows, and may include user‑requirement studies, comparative assessments of existing platforms, or design recommendations. Materials that are purely marketing brochures, product advertisements, or discuss unrelated consumer collaboration apps are not relevant.

title: Electric Bike Incentives
description: What financial incentives are available for purchasing electric bicycles in major European cities?
narrative: The user is looking for up‑to‑date information on subsidies, tax credits, rebates, or any other monetary support offered by municipal or national governments in Europe for buying e‑bikes. Relevant documents should describe the type of incentive, the amount or percentage covered, eligibility criteria, application procedures, and any geographic or temporal restrictions. Articles that merely mention e‑bikes without discussing financial assistance are not relevant.

title: Quantum Advantage Benchmarks
description: What benchmark tasks are currently used to demonstrate quantum advantage over classical computers?
narrative: The user is seeking recent (e.g., 2019‑present) scholarly sources that describe specific benchmark problems, experimental implementations, and performance comparisons illustrating quantum advantage. Relevant documents should detail the nature of the benchmark (e.g., sampling, optimization, simulation), the quantum hardware or algorithm employed, and quantitative results that show superiority to the best known classical approaches.

title: Window Tint Regulations
description: What are the legal limits for automotive window tint darkness in European countries?
narrative: The user seeks authoritative information on statutory limits for vehicle window tinting across Europe, including permitted visible light transmission percentages, differences between front, side, and rear windows, and any regional variations or enforcement guidelines. Relevant documents should detail legal standards, official regulations, or comparative tables of limits, not merely marketing or product descriptions.

title: Solar Incentives
description: What federal and state tax credits and rebate programs are currently available for residential solar panel installations in the United States?
narrative: The user seeks comprehensive, up-to‑date information on monetary incentives for homeowners who want to install photovoltaic solar systems. Relevant material must describe the federal Investment Tax Credit (including its percentage and expiration), state‑level tax credits, cash rebates, utility‑company incentives, eligibility requirements, application processes, and any deadline constraints. Documents focusing on commercial solar projects, other renewable technologies, or historical incentive data that are no longer in effect are not relevant.

title: Collaborative Car Programs
description: What are the advantages, conditions, and consumer protections associated with employee‑collaborator vehicle programs offered by European automotive groups?
narrative: The user seeks a detailed overview of collaborative/employee car resale schemes (e.g., Stellantis, Toyota) covering eligibility criteria, typical discount levels, vehicle age and mileage limits, warranty and service provisions, purchase procedures, and applicable consumer rights. Relevant material must describe how these programs operate, the benefits they provide to both the employee and buyer, and any geographic or legal restrictions. General marketing or unrelated automotive content that does not address these program specifics is non‑relevant.

title: Renewable Energy Incentives
description: What federal and state financial incentives are currently available for residential solar panel installations in the United States?
narrative: The user is seeking up-to-date information on monetary programs that support homeowners who install solar photovoltaic systems. Relevant documents should describe federal tax credits, state rebates, utility‑level incentives, eligibility criteria, application processes, and any recent changes to these programs. Materials focused on non‑residential projects, unrelated energy sources, or incentives outside the United States are not relevant.

title: Quantum Error Mitigation
description: What techniques are currently used to mitigate errors in near‑term quantum computers?
narrative: The user is seeking an overview of practical error mitigation strategies applicable to noisy intermediate‑scale quantum (NISQ) devices, including both algorithmic methods (e.g., zero‑noise extrapolation, probabilistic error cancellation) and hardware‑level approaches (e.g., dynamical decoupling, calibrated pulse shaping). Relevant documents should describe the principles, implementation challenges, and performance trade‑offs of these techniques, and may reference recent experimental results or benchmark studies.

title: EU Offshore Wind Policy
description: What recent European Union policies promote the development of offshore wind energy?
narrative: The user is seeking authoritative information on EU-level legislative and regulatory measures that support offshore wind projects, including funding programs, permitting processes, targets, and incentive schemes introduced after 2020. Relevant documents should discuss specific EU directives, regulations, or funding mechanisms, outline objectives or timelines for offshore wind capacity, and explain how these policies affect developers and member states. Materials focusing solely on national policies, unrelated renewable sources, or pre‑2020 initiatives are not relevant.

title: Renewable Energy Incentives
description: What are the current financial incentives and tax credits available for residential solar panel installations in Europe?
narrative: The user wants up-to-date information on governmental and EU‑level subsidies, tax rebates, and grant programs that support homeowners installing solar photovoltaic systems. Relevant documents must describe specific incentive amounts, eligibility criteria, geographic coverage, and application procedures.

title: AI Wage Effects
description: How will the adoption of generative AI tools affect wage inequality across different occupational groups over the next decade?
narrative: The user is seeking a comprehensive, evidence‑based overview of the projected impact of generative AI on wage structures, focusing on variations by occupation, sector, and skill level. Relevant documents should include peer‑reviewed economic research, government labor statistics, industry reports, and reputable think‑tank analyses that provide quantitative forecasts, longitudinal data, or rigorous case‑study evidence covering the period up to ten years ahead. Opinion pieces, anecdotal blog posts, and sources lacking methodological detail are not considered relevant.

title: Sustainable Urban Gardening
description: What are the best practices for a city resident to create a low‑maintenance, eco‑friendly garden on a balcony?
narrative: The user wants actionable guidance on establishing a sustainable balcony garden that requires minimal upkeep. Relevant documents should describe suitable plant species, soil mixes, watering techniques, container choices, space‑saving designs, and environmentally friendly practices such as composting or rainwater harvesting. Materials focusing on indoor or large‑scale agriculture, ornamental indoor décor unrelated to food production, or general gardening without a focus on small urban spaces are not relevant.

title: Urban Heat Islands
description: How do urban heat islands affect local climate patterns and public health in mid‑size cities?
narrative: The user seeks scholarly and governmental sources that explain the mechanisms by which concrete and asphalt concentrations raise temperatures in urban areas, quantify the impact on micro‑climate (e.g., temperature differentials, precipitation changes) and assess associated health outcomes such as heat‑related morbidity. Relevant documents should discuss measurement methods, mitigation strategies (green roofs, tree canopy, reflective surfaces), and include case studies from cities with populations between 100,000 and 1 million. Materials focused solely on rural heat dynamics, unrelated climate topics, or anecdotal blog posts are not relevant.

title: Urban Heat Islands
description: What are the most effective mitigation strategies for reducing urban heat island effects in large cities?
narrative: The user seeks recent, peer‑reviewed research that evaluates, compares, or proposes practical measures (e.g., green roofs, cool pavements, urban canopy expansion) to lessen the intensity of urban heat islands. Relevant documents should discuss empirical results, case studies, or modeling of temperature reductions in metropolitan areas, and be dated within the last ten years.

title: EU Offshore Wind
description: What recent EU policy measures have been introduced to promote offshore wind farms?
narrative: The user is looking for documents that describe European Union legislation, incentives, and regulatory frameworks enacted since 2020 to support offshore wind energy development. Relevant material should include specific policy texts, implementation status, funding mechanisms, case studies, and any reported environmental or economic impacts.

title: Offshore Wind Incentives
description: What federal and state policy incentives currently support the development of offshore wind farms in the Northeastern United States?
narrative: The user is looking for recent (2023‑2024) information on financial and regulatory incentives—such as tax credits, grants, feed‑in tariffs, permitting exemptions, and renewable portfolio standards—that apply to offshore wind projects in the U.S. Northeast (e.g., New York, New Jersey, Massachusetts, Connecticut, and the Atlantic coast of New England). Relevant documents should describe specific programs, eligibility criteria, funding amounts, and legislative or administrative actions that affect project planning and investment decisions.

title: Battery Recycling Policy
description: What are the current regulations and best practices for recycling lithium‑ion batteries in the United States?
narrative: The user seeks authoritative information on U.S. federal and state regulations, industry standards, and recommended procedures for the collection, transport, and processing of spent lithium‑ion batteries. Relevant material should cover legal requirements, safety and environmental guidelines, recent legislative updates, and effective recycling methods, while excluding generic consumer tips, unrelated battery chemistries, or non‑U.S. policies.

title: Copper Network Decommission
description: What policies, technical guidelines, and best‑practice examples exist for decommissioning copper telephone networks and transitioning to fiber in France?
narrative: The user wants detailed, authoritative information on how the French copper telephone infrastructure is being retired and replaced by fiber optics. Relevant material should cover regulatory directives, operator implementation plans, technical steps (e.g., removal, repurposing of ducts), timelines, stakeholder responsibilities, consumer impact mitigation, and documented case studies of successful transitions.

title: Certification Differences
description: What are the key differences between the various green electricity certification schemes available in France?
narrative: The user wants a comparative overview of French green electricity certification labels (e.g., VertVolt, Garanties d'Origine, Eco-labels), including the criteria each scheme uses, how they are verified, and their relative credibility. Relevant documents should explain the certification standards, any regulatory backing, and how they affect a consumer’s ability to choose truly renewable energy. Documents that merely market specific suppliers or describe subscription processes are not relevant.

title: Climate Migration Housing
description: How are climate‑induced migration patterns influencing urban housing markets in major coastal cities?
narrative: The user seeks information on the relationship between climate‑driven population movements and housing market dynamics in large coastal urban areas. Relevant documents should discuss climate‑related displacement (e.g., sea‑level rise, extreme weather), migration trends, and their impacts on housing demand, prices, availability, policy responses, or urban planning in coastal megacities. Studies, reports, or analyses that combine environmental migration data with real‑estate market effects are considered on‑topic, while sources focusing solely on climate change without a migration component, or on housing markets without a climate‑migration link, are not relevant.

title: Ancient Celtic Diet
description: What foods did the historical Gauls consume, and how did their diet differ across regions and social classes?
narrative: The user is researching the dietary habits of the Gauls during the Iron Age, seeking scholarly sources that describe staple foods, preparation methods, regional variations, and differences between elite and common populations. Relevant documents should provide archaeological evidence (e.g., plant remains, animal bones, cooking vessels), analysis of ancient texts or inscriptions, and peer‑reviewed interpretations of these data. Materials focused solely on modern reenactments, fictional portrayals, or contemporary restaurants named after Gauls are not relevant.

title: Coral Bleaching Trends
description: What have been the trends in coral reef bleaching in the Caribbean caused by rising sea temperatures over the last ten years?
narrative: The user is looking for recent, scientific information (approximately the past decade) on the frequency, severity, and drivers of coral bleaching events in Caribbean reefs. Relevant material includes peer‑reviewed articles, government or NGO monitoring reports, and credible datasets that discuss sea‑temperature anomalies, bleaching observations, ecological impacts, and any reported mitigation or restoration efforts. Documents focused on unrelated regions, non‑scientific commentary, or lacking clear temporal or causal analysis are not relevant.

title: Urban Noise Pollution
description: What are the most effective strategies cities have implemented to reduce urban noise pollution?
narrative: The user wants detailed information on municipal policies, engineering solutions, and community initiatives that have demonstrably lowered ambient noise levels in urban environments, favoring case studies, quantitative results, and comparative evaluations of effectiveness.

title: Microplastic Impact
description: How do microplastics affect the health and behavior of marine mammal populations worldwide?
narrative: The user seeks comprehensive, peer‑reviewed information on the physiological, ecological, and behavioral consequences of microplastic ingestion and exposure in marine mammals such as whales, dolphins, and seals. Relevant documents should present recent scientific findings, include quantitative data (e.g., concentration levels, health metrics), discuss geographic variation, and consider both direct (ingestion) and indirect (food‑web) exposure pathways. Sources focusing on other taxa, non‑marine environments, or solely on macro‑plastic debris are not relevant.

title: Solar Incentive Overview
description: What federal tax credits and state-level incentives are available for residential solar panel installations in the United States in 2024?
narrative: The user seeks current, detailed information on financial incentives that support the adoption of residential solar PV systems, including the federal Investment Tax Credit, state rebate programs, utility‑offered incentives, eligibility criteria, monetary amounts, application procedures, and any relevant deadlines. Relevant documents should be authoritative (e.g., government agency publications, reputable energy policy organizations, or utility program guides) and must explicitly describe these incentives for the 2024 tax year. General marketing material, opinion pieces, or outdated information are not relevant.

title: Renewable Energy Policies
description: What major renewable energy policy initiatives were implemented by the European Union between 2015 and 2020?
narrative: The user wants detailed information on EU-wide renewable energy policies enacted during the period 2015‑2020, including legislation, targets, funding mechanisms, and implementation measures. Relevant documents should describe specific EU directives, regulations, or programs, provide dates and objectives, explain how member states are required to comply, and discuss the outcomes or progress reported for these initiatives.

title: Renault EV Battery Recycling
description: What are Renault's policies, procedures, and partnerships for recycling the batteries of its electric vehicles, and how do they compare with industry best‑practice standards?
narrative: The user seeks comprehensive information on Renault's battery‑end‑of‑life strategy, including any corporate programs, third‑party collaborations, recycling rates, regulatory compliance, and cost structures. Relevant documents must explicitly describe Renault's recycling initiatives, quantitative results, or comparative analyses with other manufacturers. Materials that only detail vehicle specifications, sales data, or unrelated financial information are not relevant.

title: AI Ethics Frameworks
description: What are the most comprehensive frameworks for governing ethical AI deployment in enterprise environments?
narrative: The user is looking for detailed, authoritative sources that define, compare, and evaluate complete ethical AI governance frameworks suitable for large organizations. Relevant documents should describe the underlying principles, risk‑assessment procedures, compliance mechanisms, implementation guidelines, and include real‑world case studies. Acceptable content includes scholarly articles, industry whitepapers, or official standards such as IEEE Ethically Aligned Design, the EU AI Act, OECD AI Principles, and similar models, with critical analysis of their applicability, strengths, and limitations. Sources that merely mention AI without addressing governance, provide superficial overviews, or lack concrete framework details are considered non‑relevant.

title: Remote Work Retail
description: What is the impact of the widespread adoption of remote work on sales performance of suburban shopping centers?
narrative: The user is looking for evidence‑based analyses, market reports, or academic studies that examine how the increase in remote‑working arrangements (especially since 2020) has changed consumer foot traffic, sales revenue, tenant mix, and overall economic health of suburban retail locations. Relevant documents should provide quantitative data (e.g., sales trends, foot‑traffic counts), discuss underlying mechanisms (e.g., reduced commuting, shift to online shopping, changes in discretionary spending), and ideally compare pre‑pandemic and post‑pandemic periods. Sources that focus on urban core retail or unrelated industries are not relevant.

title: Remote Work Commuting
description: How has the rise of remote work affected daily commuting patterns and transportation mode choice in major European cities?
narrative: The user seeks recent research, statistics, and analysis that explain changes in commuting frequency, peak travel times, and the shift between car, public transit, cycling, and walking since remote work became widespread. Relevant documents should discuss urban areas in Europe, provide quantitative data (e.g., percentage reductions in vehicle trips), and cover the time frame from 2019 onward, while excluding general car sales or rental advertisements.

title: Cannabis Road Impact
description: What are the comparative effects of cannabis and alcohol consumption on motor‑vehicle crash risk and legal penalties in France?
narrative: The user is seeking a comprehensive, evidence‑based overview that compares how cannabis and alcohol influence driver safety, including statistical risk multipliers, accident frequency, and demographic patterns, as well as the corresponding legal thresholds and sanctions in French law. Relevant material should contain epidemiological studies, official statistics, policy analyses, or scholarly reviews that directly address the relative danger of each substance while driving and explain how the legal system differentiates between them. Documents that only list the legal limits without context, or that discuss unrelated topics such as film reviews, victim support organisations, or generic traffic accident news, are not relevant.

title: Solar Battery Research
description: What are the latest advances in electrochemical storage systems for grid‑scale solar power?
narrative: The user wants recent (2020‑2024) scholarly information on electrochemical battery technologies—such as lithium‑ion, flow, solid‑state, and emerging chemistries—optimized for large‑scale solar photovoltaic integration. Relevant documents should present performance data, cost assessments, scalability analyses, and real‑world deployment case studies. Sources that only cover solar panel technology, non‑electrochemical storage (e.g., pumped hydro, compressed air), or promotional/marketing material are not relevant.

title: Renewable Energy Policy
description: What are the recent policy incentives and regulatory frameworks supporting solar and wind power deployment in the European Union?
narrative: The user is seeking authoritative information on EU-level policy measures introduced in the past five years that encourage the adoption of solar and wind energy. Relevant documents should describe specific legislation, funding programs, tax incentives, or grid integration standards, and may include official EU publications, policy analyses, or reputable news coverage. Materials focusing on unrelated topics, such as local weather forecasts, obituary listings, or non‑EU jurisdictions, are not relevant.

title: Carbon Pricing Effects
description: How have recent carbon pricing mechanisms affected the electricity generation mix in European countries?
narrative: The user seeks an analytical overview of the impact of newly implemented carbon pricing schemes (e.g., EU ETS reforms, national carbon taxes) on the share of renewable versus fossil-fuel electricity generation across European nations. Relevant documents should provide data or studies that compare generation mixes before and after the pricing changes, discuss policy details, and assess measurable outcomes such as emission reductions or shifts in market share. Sources focusing solely on consumer energy vouchers, dispute resolution tools, or unrelated French energy aid programs are not relevant.

title: Solar Tax Incentives
description: What are the current federal tax incentives available for U.S. homeowners who install residential solar panels?
narrative: The user is looking for comprehensive, up‑to‑date information on the United States federal tax benefits that apply to residential solar photovoltaic installations. Relevant documents should describe the amount of the Investment Tax Credit (ITC), eligibility criteria, how to claim the credit on federal tax returns, any recent legislative changes, and timelines for when the credit phases down or expires. Sources that only mention state or local incentives, general renewable energy policy, or unrelated tax topics are not considered relevant.

title: Renewable Energy Incentives
description: What financing programs are available for residential solar panel installations in the United States in 2024?
narrative: The user wants current, detailed information on federal, state, and local incentives—such as tax credits, rebates, grants, and loan programs—specifically for homeowners installing solar photovoltaic systems in 2024. Relevant material must list eligibility requirements, monetary values, application steps, and deadlines. General solar technology advice, unrelated countries, or outdated programs are not relevant.

title: Cold War Espionage
description: What were the primary methods used by Soviet intelligence agencies to infiltrate Western scientific institutions during the 1970s?
narrative: The user seeks detailed historical information about Soviet espionage tactics aimed at Western academia and research labs in the 1970s, focusing on recruitment, tradecraft, and case studies of known infiltrations. Relevant documents should describe specific methods (e.g., honey traps, academic conferences, forged credentials), provide concrete examples of scientists or institutions compromised, and discuss the broader impact on scientific collaboration and security policies of the era. Sources limited to general culinary content or unrelated recipes are not relevant.

title: Remote Work Commuting
description: How has the rise of remote work affected daily commuting patterns and traffic congestion in major U.S. cities?
narrative: The user is looking for recent (approximately 2018‑2023) analyses, studies, or reports that examine changes in commuter volume, peak‑hour traffic flow, and related environmental impacts resulting from increased remote work. Relevant documents include scholarly articles, government transportation statistics, reputable news investigations, and industry white papers that provide quantitative data or well‑supported qualitative insights about shifts in commuting behavior in large metropolitan areas.

title: AI Hiring Ethics
description: What ethical considerations and best practices should organizations follow when using AI-driven tools for recruitment?
narrative: The user is seeking comprehensive information on the ethical challenges of AI in hiring, including bias detection and mitigation, transparency, privacy, legal compliance, and recommendations for responsible implementation. Relevant results should discuss scholarly analyses, industry guidelines, case studies of AI hiring systems, and policy frameworks. Documents that merely describe general employment services or unrelated financial aids are not relevant.

title: Solar Panel Incentives
description: What financial incentives or subsidies are available for households to install solar photovoltaic systems in European countries?
narrative: The user seeks up-to-date information on government or regional programs that provide grants, tax credits, low‑interest loans, or feed‑in tariffs to residential owners for solar PV installation across Europe. Relevant documents should describe the type of incentive, eligibility criteria, amount or percentage of support, application process, and any recent changes (e.g., 2022‑2024). Materials focusing on commercial installations, non‑European regions, or unrelated energy technologies are not relevant.

title: Renewable Energy Storage
description: What are the most efficient large‑scale battery technologies for integrating renewable electricity into the power grid?
narrative: The user is seeking comprehensive, up‑to‑date information on high‑capacity energy storage solutions that can balance intermittent renewable generation (solar, wind) with demand. Relevant documents should discuss and compare technologies such as lithium‑ion, flow batteries, solid‑state batteries, metal‑air, and emerging concepts, providing data on round‑trip efficiency, energy and power density, capital and operational costs, lifespan, scalability, safety, and environmental impact. The focus is on large‑scale (grid‑level) applications; laboratory‑scale or purely theoretical designs without performance metrics are not relevant. Preference is given to sources that include recent (last 5 years) empirical evaluations, case studies, or meta‑analyses.

title: Urban Heat Islands
description: How have urban heat islands influenced nighttime temperature trends in major U.S. cities over the past decade?
narrative: The user is seeking scholarly and authoritative sources that analyze the impact of urban heat islands on nighttime temperatures in large U.S. metropolitan areas from roughly 2013 to 2023. Relevant documents include peer‑reviewed journal articles, government or agency reports, and reputable datasets that present quantitative temperature trends, compare urban versus rural baselines, and discuss underlying mechanisms. Materials focusing solely on daytime temperatures, cities outside the United States, or time periods outside the last ten years are not relevant.

title: Renewable Energy Policies
description: What are the most effective policy mechanisms worldwide for accelerating the deployment of renewable energy technologies?
narrative: The user seeks comprehensive, up-to-date information on policy instruments (e.g., subsidies, feed‑in tariffs, renewable portfolio standards, carbon pricing, tax incentives) that have demonstrably increased renewable energy capacity in various countries. Relevant documents should describe the design, implementation, and outcomes of these policies, include quantitative results (e.g., added megawatts, percentage of energy mix), and discuss contextual factors such as market structure, regulatory environment, and socio‑economic impacts. Sources focusing on a single case study without broader analysis, opinion pieces without evidence, or unrelated topics (e.g., agriculture, cooking) are not relevant.

title: Carbon Pricing Comparison
description: What are the key differences between carbon pricing mechanisms employed in Europe and those used in North America?
narrative: The user is seeking a comprehensive overview that contrasts European and North American approaches to pricing carbon emissions. Relevant documents should describe the design, scope, and performance of schemes such as the EU Emissions Trading System, the Canadian federal carbon tax, and U.S. regional cap‑and‑trade programs (e.g., RGGI, California). The content should include policy objectives, coverage sectors, price levels, market dynamics, and any reported environmental or economic outcomes, preferably with data or analysis published after 2020. Documents that only mention one region, lack substantive comparison, or focus on unrelated climate policies are not relevant.
